member_bio: Crypto Tax Lawyer at Solstice Law
message: Hi everyone — I’m based in Toronto, Canada, and I run one of the only law firms in Canada focused specifically on crypto and digital asset tax.My practice is often brought in by accountants, tax advisors, and other professionals when a client’s file moves beyond routine compliance and into more complex planning, audit, or dispute territory. We advise on the Canadian and cross-border tax issues affecting founders, investors, protocols, exchanges, Bitcoin ATM businesses, and other digital asset companies.We regularly assist with matters such as offshore and domestic tax planning, entity structuring, token launches, departure tax, residency issues, CRA audits, GST/HST and excise tax, voluntary disclosures, and tax litigation. We also work on complex crypto files involving DeFi, token transactions, cross-border holdings, foreign entities, and clients relocating to lower-tax jurisdictions.Our role is often to support the accountant and help protect the client where the legal, tax, and evidentiary issues become higher risk or more technical. Some of our clients have included teams such as Helius, Tensor Labs/Vector, and Phygitals.I’m always glad to be a resource for accountants dealing with files that require deeper analysis, structuring, or audit and litigation support. Happy to connect!

But i would still say it is a field where you need to get new knowledge all the time, as you have to interpret the client business and accounting rules to figure out what to do. member_bio: Ex-Accountant @kpk/ Polygon labs/ Modex (Open for New Opportunities)
message: Hey everyone — Vamsi Reddy here. Senior Crypto Accountant, based in India, open to remote opportunities globally.I didn't start in crypto. I started my career in 2011, the old-fashioned way — processing invoices at EXL, then audit at KPMG, then finance ops across a few fast-moving companies like Consero (CGS). Solid, traditional, unglamorous.Then in 2022, I joined Polygon Labs.And everything I thought I knew about accounting got stress-tested. No software handled what we were doing. No playbook existed. You had to reconcile multi-sig wallets against chain explorers manually, classify staking rewards and bridge transactions in real time, and build internal controls for assets that moved while you slept.I loved it.Since then, I've built out crypto accounting functions for:• Polygon Labs — daily digital asset accounting across Ethereum, Polygon & Gnosis treasury wallets  • KPK Foundation — 50+ wallets, 6,000+ monthly transactions, full AP/subledger ownership • Modex — end-to-end crypto + fiat accounting, GL, audits, and leadership reportingWhat excites me right now is the same thing that's probably on everyone's radar here — how do you build accounting infrastructure for assets that traditional ERPs were never designed to handle? That's the problem I want to keep solving.I'm currently looking for my next opportunity — ideally with a team that's serious about getting their financial operations right, not just "good enough."If you're a CFO, Finance Lead, Accountant or building a Web3 finance team — I'd love to connect. Always happy to talk Defi, treasury, reconciliation strategy, or digital asset accounting & AI automation.
